meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve tutorial hell (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome tutorial hell. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Break out of tutorial hell by implementing real-world analytics. Master Antigravity and Claude Code in this expert-led, intermediate-level case study.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop watching and start measuring. By integrating lightweight analytics, you transform your project from a static demo into a data-driven application. Use Antigravity to scaffold your telemetry and Claude Code to automate the deployment, proving your app's value through real user behavior metrics.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks iterating on code that nobody sees. You feel the crushing weight of 'tutorial hell'—that deceptive cycle where you follow instructions perfectly but feel incapable of building anything original. The screen glows with syntax, but your portfolio remains empty of impact.

Real engineering isn't about perfectly following a guide; it is about shipping features that solve problems you can measure. Today, we bridge the gap between 'student code' and 'production-ready software' by implementing a robust analytics layer. This is the moment you stop being a consumer of content and start becoming a builder of systems.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the one action that proves your app provides value. Analytics without a goal is just noise.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and suggest the top 3 user interactions that define successful engagement for a SaaS MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Identify key user event triggers in the current directory'

steps.title: Scaffold the Analytics Provider
steps.description: Use Antigravity to inject a clean, modular analytics wrapper that abstracts the provider logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a clean, singleton analytics wrapper for a React app that supports PostHog and local logging.

steps.title: Instrument Key Components
steps.description: Manually wrap your call-to-action buttons with event triggers to capture user intent.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my primary CTA buttons to include click-tracking events using the analytics wrapper created in step 2.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file src/components/Button.tsx --instruction 'Add event tracking to onClick handler'

steps.title: Automate Telemetry Testing
steps.description: Ensure your tracking actually fires by writing automated tests for your analytics events.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test suite using Vitest to verify that analytics events are dispatched correctly on button clicks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create vitest suite for analytics event verification'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate Data
steps.description: Push your changes and verify that the data is appearing in your dashboard in real-time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Check my deployment configuration and advise on how to verify data flow in a production-like environment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Validate analytics integration and deployment logs'

pro_tips: Never track everything. Focus on high-intent events that correlate with user retention.
Use local development logging to verify event payloads before pushing to production.
Anonymize all user data at the edge to maintain privacy standards without sacrificing insights.
Connect your analytics dashboard to your README.md; showing data proves your app is 'live'.
Treat your analytics schema like API documentation—keep it versioned and typed.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, share your dashboard (anonymized) on LinkedIn to show employers you understand how to measure product success.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in a vacuum. Move from tutorial-taker to data-driven engineer today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
